set -euo pipefail

resolve_script_dir() {
  local source="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
  while [[ -L "$source" ]]; do
    local dir
    dir="$(cd -P "$(dirname "$source")" && pwd)"
    source="$(readlink "$source")"
    [[ "$source" != /* ]] && source="$dir/$source"
  done
  cd -P "$(dirname "$source")" && pwd
}

# Use HELIX_ROOT if set (e.g., by bin/helix plugin wrapper), otherwise
# resolve from this script's location.
if [[ -n "${HELIX_ROOT:-}" ]]; then
  library_root="$HELIX_ROOT"
else
  script_dir="$(resolve_script_dir)"
  library_root="$(cd "$script_dir/.." && pwd)"
fi
HELIX_VERSION="0.3.3"
helix_docs_root="${HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT:-$library_root/workflows}"
target_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)"

# DDx bead-backed tracker settings
tracker_dir="${DDX_BEAD_DIR:-${HELIX_TRACKER_DIR:-${target_root}/.ddx}}"
tracker_file="${tracker_dir}/beads.jsonl"
export DDX_BEAD_DIR="$tracker_dir"

canonicalize_path() {
  local path="$1"
  local dir
  dir="$(cd "$(dirname "$path")" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)" || return 1
  printf '%s/%s\n' "$dir" "$(basename "$path")"
}

path_is_within_root() {
  local path="$1"
  local root="$2"
  local resolved_path
  local resolved_root

  resolved_path="$(canonicalize_path "$path")" || return 1
  resolved_root="$(cd "$root" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)" || return 1

  case "$resolved_path" in
    "$resolved_root" | "$resolved_root"/*) return 0 ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac
}

normalize_agent_name() {
  # Normalize DDx-embedded-harness aliases to the canonical HELIX name "agent".
  # ddx-agent, agent, embedded, and legacy forge all map to "agent".
  # All other harness names pass through unchanged.
  case "${1:-}" in
    ddx-agent|agent|embedded|forge) printf 'agent' ;;
    *) printf '%s' "${1:-}" ;;
  esac
}

agent="$(normalize_agent_name "${HELIX_AGENT:-codex}")"
agent_model="${HELIX_MODEL:-}"
agent_effort="${HELIX_EFFORT:-}"
review_every=15
force_ephemeral="${HELIX_FORCE_EPHEMERAL:-0}"
session_active=0  # 1 = reusable session exists for the current epic
max_cycles=0
auto_align="${HELIX_AUTO_ALIGN:-1}"
auto_review=1
review_threshold=100

# DDx embedded-harness alias handling.
# HELIX should accept the user-facing rename direction (`ddx-agent`) while still
# working against older DDx binaries that expose the embedded harness as `forge`.
_is_ddx_embedded_alias() {
  case "${1:-}" in
    ddx-agent|agent|embedded|forge)
      return 0
      ;;
    *)
      return 1
      ;;
  esac
}

declare -A _ddx_harness_alias_cache 2>/dev/null || true
_resolve_ddx_dispatch_harness() {
  local requested="${1:-}"
  [[ -z "$requested" ]] && return 0
  if ! _is_ddx_embedded_alias "$requested"; then
    printf '%s' "$requested"
    return 0
  fi
  if [[ -v "_ddx_harness_alias_cache[$requested]" ]]; then
    printf '%s' "${_ddx_harness_alias_cache[$requested]}"
    return 0
  fi

  local resolved=""
  if command -v ddx &>/dev/null; then
    local _list
    _list="$(ddx agent list --json 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ -n "$_list" ]]; then
      local candidate _avail
      for candidate in agent embedded forge; do
        _avail="$(printf '%s' "$_list" | ddx jq -r --arg h "$candidate" '.[] | select(.name == $h and .available == true) | .name' 2>/dev/null || true)"
        if [[ -n "$_avail" ]]; then
          resolved="$_avail"
          break
        fi
      done
    fi
  fi

  if [[ -z "$resolved" ]]; then
    case "$requested" in
      ddx-agent|agent)
        resolved="agent"
        ;;
      embedded)
        resolved="embedded"
        ;;
      forge)
        resolved="forge"
        ;;
    esac
  fi

  _ddx_harness_alias_cache["$requested"]="$resolved"
  printf '%s' "$resolved"
}

# Adversarial agent rotation: reasoning stages use the primary agent,
# mechanical stages (build, polish, check) use the alternate agent.
# This ensures different models review each other's work.
# Set HELIX_ALT_AGENT=none to explicitly disable auto-detection.
if [[ "${HELIX_ALT_AGENT:-auto}" != "auto" ]]; then
  alt_agent="$(normalize_agent_name "${HELIX_ALT_AGENT}")"
  [[ "$alt_agent" == "none" ]] && alt_agent=""
elif [[ "${agent}" == "codex" ]] && command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  alt_agent="claude"
elif [[ "${agent}" == "claude" ]] && command -v codex >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  alt_agent="codex"
else
  # For other agents (e.g. ddx-agent / agent), try ddx agent list to find a suitable alternate.
  # Prefer claude or codex as the adversarial reviewer when available.
  alt_agent=""
  if command -v ddx &>/dev/null; then
    _alt_list="$(ddx agent list --json 2>/dev/null || true)"
    for _alt_pref in claude codex; do
      if [[ "$_alt_pref" != "$agent" ]]; then
        _alt_avail="$(printf '%s' "$_alt_list" | ddx jq -r --arg h "$_alt_pref" '.[] | select(.name == $h and .available == true) | .name' 2>/dev/null || true)"
        if [[ -n "$_alt_avail" ]]; then
          alt_agent="$_alt_pref"
          break
        fi
      fi
    done
  fi
fi
# review_agent kept as alias for backward compat with --review-agent flag
review_agent="${HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT:-$alt_agent}"
dry_run=0
quiet=0
summary=0
total_tokens=0

# Session log — stderr is copied to a log file for post-hoc inspection.
# We use a background tee with explicit PID tracking so the tee process
# is cleaned up on exit.  This prevents $() subshells (used by the test
# harness) from hanging on an orphan tee that holds the captured pipe open.
helix_log_dir="${target_root}/.helix-logs"
mkdir -p "$helix_log_dir"
helix_log="${helix_log_dir}/helix-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log"
exec 3>&2
exec 2> >(tee -a "$helix_log" >&3)
_helix_tee_pid=$!
_helix_cleanup() {
  exec 2>&3 3>&-  # restore stderr, close fd 3
  if [[ -n "${_helix_tee_pid:-}" ]]; then
    kill "$_helix_tee_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    wait "$_helix_tee_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}
trap _helix_cleanup EXIT

# Summary-mode helpers: concise output to fd 3, verbose to fd 2 (log only).
# In normal mode both go to stderr (which tee's to log anyway).

# Current line count in the log file (for referencing ranges)
log_line() {
  wc -l < "$helix_log" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' '
}

# Write a concise one-liner to the user (fd 3) — always visible
summary_line() {
  local fmt="$1"; shift
  # shellcheck disable=SC2059
  printf "helix: ${fmt}\n" "$@" >&3
}

# Write verbose detail — goes to log file only in summary mode, stderr in normal mode
verbose_line() {
  local fmt="$1"; shift
  if (( summary )); then
    # Write directly to log file only (skip fd 3 / user-visible stderr)
    # shellcheck disable=SC2059
    printf "helix: ${fmt}\n" "$@" >> "$helix_log"
  else
    # shellcheck disable=SC2059
    printf "helix: ${fmt}\n" "$@" >&2
  fi
}


usage() {
  cat <<EOF
Usage:
  helix run [--agent <harness>] [--review-every N] [--max-cycles N] [--no-auto-align] [--no-auto-review] [--summary] [--dry-run]
  helix input [--agent <harness>] [--autonomy low|medium|high] "natural language request"
  helix frame [--agent <harness>] [scope]
  helix build [--agent <harness>] [selector]
  helix check [--agent <harness>] [scope]
  helix align [--agent <harness>] [scope]
  helix backfill [--agent <harness>] [scope]
  helix design [--agent <harness>] [--rounds N] [scope]
  helix polish [--agent <harness>] [--rounds N] [scope]
  helix doctor [--fix]
  helix start [helix run options...]
  helix stop
  helix status [--json]
  helix next
  helix review [--agent <harness>] [scope]
  helix experiment [--agent <harness>] [--close] [issue-id|goal]
  helix evolve [--agent <harness>] [--scope S] [--artifact ID] [--from source] "requirement"
  helix commit [--agent <harness>] [issue-id]
  helix triage "Title" [--type task|epic|bug|chore] [ddx bead create options...]
  ddx bead <command> [args...]
  helix help

Commands:
  run         Loop over ready HELIX work, then check whether to continue
  doctor      Check installation health; --fix auto-repairs plugin symlinks and skills
  start       Launch helix run as a background process (PID file written)
  stop        Kill the background helix run cleanly
  input       Accept sparse intent and shape governed HELIX work
  frame       Create or refine product vision, PRD, and feature specs
  build       Run one bounded implementation pass
  check       Run the bounded HELIX queue-health check
  align       Run reconcile-alignment for a scope (default: repo)
  backfill    Run documentation backfill for a scope (default: repo)
  design      Create a comprehensive design document through iterative refinement
  polish      Refine issues before implementation (dedup, coverage, criteria)
  next        Show recommended next issue
  review      Fresh-eyes review of recent work
  experiment  Run one experiment iteration for metric-driven optimization
  evolve      Thread a new requirement through the artifact stack
  commit      Commit with HELIX format, run build gate, push, close issue
  triage      Create a bead via ddx bead create

Options:
  --agent           Agent harness to use: codex, claude, ddx-agent,
                    embedded, or any harness from \`ddx agent list\`
                    (default: ${agent})
  --claude          Shorthand for --agent claude
  --codex           Shorthand for --agent codex
  --ddx-agent       Shorthand for --agent ddx-agent
  --embedded        Shorthand for --agent embedded
  --forge           Deprecated alias for the embedded DDx agent harness
  --model           Explicit model override to pass through to DDx / the harness
  --effort          Reasoning effort: low, medium, high, max (defaults vary by action)
  --review-every    Run alignment every N completed implementation cycles
  --max-cycles      Stop after N completed implementation cycles
  --no-auto-align   Do not auto-run alignment when check returns ALIGN
  --no-auto-review  Do not run fresh-eyes review after each implementation
  --review-threshold N  Skip review for changes below N lines (default: 100)
  --review-agent    Use a different agent for reviews (default: adversarial alternate)
  --dry-run         Print the agent command instead of executing it
  --quiet, -q       Suppress progress output (command echo, prompt sizes)
  --summary, -s     Concise output with log-file line pointers (implies --quiet)

Environment:
  HELIX_AGENT          Default primary agent (codex, claude, ddx-agent,
                       or any ddx harness)
  HELIX_ALT_AGENT      Alternate agent for adversarial rotation (auto-detected)
  HELIX_AGENT_TIMEOUT  Agent timeout in seconds (default: 2700)
  HELIX_MODEL          Optional model override passed through to DDx / the harness
  HELIX_EFFORT         Optional effort override passed through to DDx / the harness
  HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT   Override the library root that contains workflows/
  HELIX_TRACKER_DIR    Override the tracker directory (default: <repo>/.ddx)

Adversarial rotation:
  Primary agent handles frame/design/evolve/align/review.
  Alternate agent handles build/polish/check.
  Concrete model selection stays with DDx or the harness unless you pass --model.

Logs:
  Session logs are written to .helix-logs/helix-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.log
  in the project root. All stderr output is captured for post-hoc inspection.

Notes:
  \`helix run\` remains a compatibility wrapper for supervisory routing, but
  build cycles delegate to \`ddx agent execute-loop --once\` when that DDx
  surface is available.
  \`helix build\` remains a compatibility wrapper for one bounded pass and
  delegates to \`ddx agent execute-bead\` after resolving a matching ready bead
  when that DDx surface is available.
  Codex runs as \`codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox exec\`.
  Any harness listed by \`ddx agent list\` can be passed to --agent.
  \`ddx-agent\`, \`embedded\`, and legacy \`forge\` are resolved against the live DDx embedded harness name.
  When HELIX dispatches through DDx, default model selection is delegated to DDx unless you pass an explicit override.
EOF
}

need_cmd() {
  if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "missing required command: $1" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

run_doctor() {
  local fix="${1:-0}"
  local ok=0 warn=0 fail=0 fixed=0

  _check() {
    local name="$1" required="${2:-1}"
    if command -v "$name" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      local ver
      ver="$("$name" --version 2>/dev/null || "$name" -V 2>/dev/null || "$name" version 2>/dev/null || echo "ok")"
      ver="$(printf '%s' "$ver" | head -1)"
      printf '  ✓  %-12s %s\n' "$name" "$ver"
      ok=$((ok + 1))
    elif (( required )); then
      printf '  ✗  %-12s MISSING (required)\n' "$name"
      fail=$((fail + 1))
    else
      printf '  -  %-12s not found (optional)\n' "$name"
      warn=$((warn + 1))
    fi
  }

  _fix_symlink() {
    local target="$1" link_path="$2" label="$3"
    if [[ -L "$link_path" ]]; then
      # Resolve both to absolute paths for comparison
      local resolved_link resolved_target
      resolved_link="$(cd "$(dirname "$link_path")" && cd "$(readlink "$link_path")" 2>/dev/null && pwd)" || resolved_link=""
      resolved_target="$(cd "$target" 2>/dev/null && pwd)" || resolved_target="$target"
      if [[ -n "$resolved_link" && "$resolved_link" == "$resolved_target" ]]; then
        printf '  ✓  %-12s %s\n' "$label" "$link_path"
        ok=$((ok + 1))
        return 0
      elif [[ -d "$link_path" ]]; then
        # Symlink resolves to a valid directory, just not the expected one
        printf '  ✓  %-12s %s\n' "$label" "$link_path"
        ok=$((ok + 1))
        return 0
      else
        if (( fix )); then
          ln -sfn "$target" "$link_path"
          printf '  ✓  %-12s %s (updated)\n' "$label" "$link_path"
          fixed=$((fixed + 1))
          return 0
        else
          printf '  ✗  %-12s %s (stale: points to %s)\n' "$label" "$link_path" "$(readlink "$link_path")"
          fail=$((fail + 1))
          return 1
        fi
      fi
    elif [[ -e "$link_path" ]]; then
      printf '  ✗  %-12s %s exists but is not a symlink\n' "$label" "$link_path"
      fail=$((fail + 1))
      return 1
    else
      if (( fix )); then
        mkdir -p "$(dirname "$link_path")"
        ln -s "$target" "$link_path"
        printf '  ✓  %-12s %s (created)\n' "$label" "$link_path"
        fixed=$((fixed + 1))
        return 0
      else
        printf '  ✗  %-12s NOT FOUND at %s\n' "$label" "$link_path"
        fail=$((fail + 1))
        return 1
      fi
    fi
  }

  printf 'HELIX Doctor\n'
  printf '============\n\n'
  printf 'Version:  helix %s\n' "$HELIX_VERSION"
  printf 'Root:     %s\n' "$library_root"
  (( fix )) && printf 'Mode:     --fix (will auto-repair)\n'
  printf '\n'

  printf 'Required:\n'
  _check bash 1
  _check git 1
  _check ddx 1

  printf '\nAgent CLIs (at least one required):\n'
  local has_agent=0
  # Use ddx agent list for discovery when available; fall back to hardcoded checks.
  if command -v ddx &>/dev/null; then
    local _agent_list
    _agent_list="$(ddx agent list --json 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ -n "$_agent_list" ]]; then
      local _name _avail
      while IFS= read -r _name; do
        _avail="$(printf '%s' "$_agent_list" | ddx jq -r --arg h "$_name" '.[] | select(.name == $h) | .available' 2>/dev/null || true)"
        if [[ "$_avail" == "true" ]]; then
          _check "$_name" 0; has_agent=1
        else
          printf '  -  %-12s not available (ddx agent list reports unavailable)\n' "$_name"
        fi
      done < <(printf '%s' "$_agent_list" | ddx jq -r '.[].name' 2>/dev/null || true)
    else
      # ddx agent list returned nothing — fall back to direct binary checks
      if command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        _check claude 0; has_agent=1
      fi
      if command -v codex >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        _check codex 0; has_agent=1
      fi
    fi
  else
    if command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      _check claude 0; has_agent=1
    fi
    if command -v codex >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      _check codex 0; has_agent=1
    fi
  fi
  if (( ! has_agent )); then
    printf '  ✗  No agent CLI found (need at least one from: ddx agent list)\n'
    fail=$((fail + 1))
  fi

  printf '\nOptional:\n'
  _check tmux 0
  _check asciinema 0
  _check hugo 0
  _check docker 0

  printf '\nWorkflows:\n'
  if [[ -d "$helix_docs_root" ]]; then
    printf '  ✓  %-12s %s\n' "workflows" "$helix_docs_root"
  else
    printf '  ✗  %-12s NOT FOUND at %s\n' "workflows" "$helix_docs_root"
    fail=$((fail + 1))
  fi

  # Skills reference .ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/ from the target repo.
  # Verify that path resolves so agents can find workflow docs.
  printf '\nPlugin path:\n'
  local plugin_dir="${target_root}/.ddx/plugins/helix"
  _fix_symlink "$library_root" "$plugin_dir" "plugin"

  printf '\nSkills:\n'
  local skill_count=0
  if [[ -d "$library_root/skills" ]]; then
    skill_count="$(find "$library_root/skills" -name SKILL.md 2>/dev/null | wc -l)"
  fi
  printf '  %-14s %s skills found in library\n' "" "$skill_count"

  # Check .agents/skills and .claude/skills symlinks in target repo
  printf '\nSkill symlinks:\n'
  local skills_dir="${library_root}/skills"
  local agents_dir="${target_root}/.agents/skills"
  local claude_dir="${target_root}/.claude/skills"
  local stale_links=0

  for skill_src in "$skills_dir"/helix-*/SKILL.md; do
    [[ -e "$skill_src" ]] || continue
    local skill_name
    skill_name="$(basename "$(dirname "$skill_src")")"
    local rel_target="../../skills/$skill_name"

    # .agents/skills/<name>
    if [[ -L "$agents_dir/$skill_name" ]]; then
      local current
      current="$(readlink "$agents_dir/$skill_name")"
      if [[ "$current" != "$rel_target" ]] && (( fix )); then
        ln -sfn "$rel_target" "$agents_dir/$skill_name"
        fixed=$((fixed + 1))
      elif [[ "$current" != "$rel_target" ]]; then
        stale_links=$((stale_links + 1))
      fi
    elif (( fix )); then
      mkdir -p "$agents_dir"
      ln -s "$rel_target" "$agents_dir/$skill_name"
      fixed=$((fixed + 1))
    else
      stale_links=$((stale_links + 1))
    fi

    # .claude/skills/<name>
    if [[ -L "$claude_dir/$skill_name" ]]; then
      local current
      current="$(readlink "$claude_dir/$skill_name")"
      if [[ "$current" != "$rel_target" ]] && (( fix )); then
        ln -sfn "$rel_target" "$claude_dir/$skill_name"
        fixed=$((fixed + 1))
      elif [[ "$current" != "$rel_target" ]]; then
        stale_links=$((stale_links + 1))
      fi
    elif (( fix )); then
      mkdir -p "$claude_dir"
      ln -s "$rel_target" "$claude_dir/$skill_name"
      fixed=$((fixed + 1))
    else
      stale_links=$((stale_links + 1))
    fi
  done

  if (( stale_links > 0 )); then
    printf '  ✗  %d missing or stale skill symlinks (run helix doctor --fix)\n' "$stale_links"
    fail=$((fail + 1))
  else
    printf '  ✓  .agents/skills and .claude/skills up to date\n'
    ok=$((ok + 1))
  fi

  printf '\nTracker:\n'
  if [[ -f "$tracker_file" ]]; then
    local bead_count
    bead_count="$(wc -l < "$tracker_file")"
    printf '  ✓  %-12s %s beads\n' "$tracker_file" "$bead_count"
  else
    printf '  -  %-12s no tracker in this directory\n' "$tracker_file"
  fi

  printf '\n'
  if (( fixed > 0 )); then
    printf 'Fixed: %d items repaired\n' "$fixed"
  fi
  if (( fail > 0 )); then
    printf 'Result: %s ok, %s warnings, %s FAILURES\n' "$ok" "$warn" "$fail"
    (( ! fix )) && printf 'Hint: run "helix doctor --fix" to auto-repair\n'
    return 1
  else
    printf 'Result: %s ok, %s warnings — ready to go\n' "$ok" "$warn"
    return 0
  fi
}

tracker_init() {
  ddx bead init >/dev/null
}

tracker_ensure() {
  need_cmd ddx

  if [[ ! -f "$tracker_file" ]]; then
    tracker_init
  fi
}

require_tracker() {
  tracker_ensure
}

# Strip non-JSON advisory lines from ddx output before piping to JSON consumers.
# Advisory lines (e.g. upgrade notices) appear before the JSON and must not
# reach ddx jq or other JSON parsers. Skips all lines until the first JSON
# delimiter ([ or {), then passes all subsequent lines unchanged. This handles
# both compact and pretty-printed JSON as well as array and object output.
# Used by tracker_exec when --json is requested.
strip_advisory() {
  awk 'found || /^[{[]/ { found=1; print }' || true
}

tracker_exec() {
  local has_json=0
  for arg in "$@"; do
    [[ "$arg" == "--json" ]] && { has_json=1; break; }
  done
  if (( has_json )); then
    ddx bead "$@" | strip_advisory
  else
    ddx bead "$@"
  fi
}

refresh_context_digest_for_bead() {
  local bead_id="$1"
  local helper="${library_root}/scripts/refresh_context_digests.py"

  [[ -f "$helper" ]] || return 0
  command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0

  python3 "$helper" \
    --apply \
    --root "$target_root" \
    --library-root "$library_root" \
    --bead "$bead_id" >/dev/null
}

tracker_create() {
  local title="" type="task" labels="" deps="" parent="" spec_id=""
  local description="" design="" acceptance="" priority=2 execution_eligible=""
  local superseded_by="" replaces=""
  local -a cmd=(ddx bead create)

  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --type)       type="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --labels)     labels="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --deps)       deps="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --parent)     parent="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --spec-id)    spec_id="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --description) description="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --design)     design="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --acceptance) acceptance="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --priority)   priority="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --execution-eligible) execution_eligible="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --superseded-by) superseded_by="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --replaces)   replaces="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --set)        cmd+=(--set "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --silent)     shift ;;
      -h|--help|help)
        tracker_exec create --help
        return 0
        ;;
      *) title="$1"; shift ;;
    esac
  done

  if [[ -z "$title" ]]; then
    echo "tracker: title required" >&2
    return 1
  fi

  cmd+=( "$title" --type "$type" --priority "$priority" )
  [[ -n "$labels" ]]      && cmd+=(--labels "$labels")
  [[ -n "$parent" ]]      && cmd+=(--parent "$parent")
  [[ -n "$description" ]] && cmd+=(--description "$description")
  [[ -n "$acceptance" ]]  && cmd+=(--acceptance "$acceptance")

  [[ -n "$spec_id" ]]     && cmd+=(--set "spec-id=$spec_id")
  [[ -n "$design" ]]      && cmd+=(--set "design=$design")
  [[ -n "$superseded_by" ]] && cmd+=(--set "superseded-by=$superseded_by")
  [[ -n "$replaces" ]]    && cmd+=(--set "replaces=$replaces")

  if [[ -z "$execution_eligible" ]]; then
    case ",${labels}," in
      *,phase:build,*|*,phase:deploy,*|*,phase:iterate,*)
        execution_eligible="true" ;;
      *)
        execution_eligible="false" ;;
    esac
  fi
  [[ -n "$execution_eligible" ]] && cmd+=(--set "execution-eligible=$execution_eligible")

  local id
  id="$("${cmd[@]}")" || return 1

  if [[ -n "$deps" ]]; then
    local dep
    for dep in ${deps//,/ }; do
      ddx bead dep add "$id" "$dep" >/dev/null || return 1
    done
  fi

  refresh_context_digest_for_bead "$id" || return 1

  printf '%s\n' "$id"
}

tracker_show() {
  tracker_exec show "$@"
}

tracker_update() {
  if [[ "${1:-}" == "--help" || "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "help" ]]; then
    tracker_exec update --help
    return 0
  fi

  local id="$1"
  shift

  local -a cmd=(ddx bead update "$id")
  local claim=0 unclaim=0 deps_to_add=""

  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --title)      cmd+=(--title "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --status)     cmd+=(--status "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --priority)   cmd+=(--priority "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --labels)     cmd+=(--labels "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --acceptance) cmd+=(--acceptance "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --assignee)   cmd+=(--assignee "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --notes)      cmd+=(--set "notes=$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --claim)      claim=1; shift ;;
      --unclaim)    unclaim=1; shift ;;
      --set)        cmd+=(--set "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --spec-id)    cmd+=(--set "spec-id=$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --design)     cmd+=(--set "design=$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --deps)       deps_to_add="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --execution-eligible) cmd+=(--set "execution-eligible=$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --superseded-by)      cmd+=(--set "superseded-by=$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --replaces)           cmd+=(--set "replaces=$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --parent)     cmd+=(--parent "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --unset)      cmd+=(--unset "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      *)            shift ;;
    esac
  done

  if (( claim )); then
    ddx bead update "$id" --claim
    return $?
  fi

  if (( unclaim )); then
    ddx bead update "$id" --unclaim
    return $?
  fi

  if ! "${cmd[@]}"; then
    return 1
  fi

  if [[ -n "$deps_to_add" ]]; then
    local dep
    for dep in ${deps_to_add//,/ }; do
      ddx bead dep add "$id" "$dep" >/dev/null || true
    done
  fi
}

normalize_tracker_issue_json() {
  local issue_json="${1:-}"
  printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -cS 'del(.updated_at)'
}

expected_tracker_issue_after_closing_sha_update() {
  local issue_json="${1:-}"
  local target_sha="${2:-}"
  printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -cS --arg sha "$target_sha" '.closing_commit_sha = $sha'
}

tracker_issue_jsonl_line() {
  local issue_id="$1"
  python3 - "$tracker_file" "$issue_id" <<'PY'
import json
import sys

path, issue_id = sys.argv[1:3]
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
    for raw in fh:
        line = raw.rstrip("\n")
        if not line.strip():
            continue
        if json.loads(line).get("id") == issue_id:
            print(line)
            raise SystemExit(0)
raise SystemExit(1)
PY
}

restore_tracker_issue_json() {
  local issue_id="$1"
  local original_issue_json="$2"
  local tmp_file=""

  tmp_file="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/helix-tracker-restore.XXXXXX")" || return 1
  if ! python3 - "$tracker_file" "$tmp_file" "$issue_id" "$original_issue_json" <<'PY'
import json
import sys

src, dst, issue_id, original_json = sys.argv[1:5]
found = False

with open(src, "r", encoding="utf-8") as inp, open(dst, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:
    for raw in inp:
        if raw.endswith("\n"):
            line = raw[:-1]
            newline = "\n"
        else:
            line = raw
            newline = ""
        if not line.strip():
            out.write(raw)
            continue
        obj = json.loads(line)
        if obj.get("id") == issue_id:
            out.write(original_json + "\n")
            found = True
        else:
            out.write(line + newline)

if not found:
    raise SystemExit(2)
PY
  then
    rm -f "$tmp_file"
    return 1
  fi

  mv -f "$tmp_file" "$tracker_file"
}

rollback_issue_closing_commit_sha_update() {
  local issue_id="$1"
  local original_issue_json="$2"
  local expected_issue_json="$3"
  local current_issue_json=""
  local current_norm=""
  local expected_norm=""

  current_issue_json="$(tracker_show "$issue_id" --json 2>/dev/null)" || {
    echo "helix: failed to reload tracker state for $issue_id after closing_commit_sha sync failure" >&2
    return 1
  }

  current_norm="$(normalize_tracker_issue_json "$current_issue_json")" || return 1
  expected_norm="$(normalize_tracker_issue_json "$expected_issue_json")" || return 1
  if [[ "$current_norm" != "$expected_norm" ]]; then
    echo "helix: refusing to roll back closing_commit_sha for $issue_id after concurrent tracker changes" >&2
    return 1
  fi

  restore_tracker_issue_json "$issue_id" "$original_issue_json" || return 1
}

tracker_close() {
  tracker_exec close "$@"
}

alignment_bead_title() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  printf 'align: %s\n' "$scope"
}

alignment_bead_acceptance() {
  printf '%s\n' \
    'Alignment review complete; all gaps classified; execution issues created for real gaps; traceability matrix produced'
}

alignment_area_labels_from_path() {
  local path="${1#./}"

  [[ "$path" == website/* ]] && printf 'area:site\n'
  [[ "$path" == docs/demos/* ]] && printf 'area:demo\n'
  [[ "$path" == docs/* ]] && printf 'area:docs\n'
  [[ "$path" == tests/* ]] && printf 'area:testing\n'
  [[ "$path" == scripts/* || "$path" == bin/* ]] && printf 'area:cli\n'
  [[ "$path" == workflows/* || "$path" == .ddx/* ]] && printf 'area:workflow\n'
  [[ "$path" == *"/artifacts/"* || "$path" == */artifacts ]] && printf 'area:artifacts\n'
}

alignment_project_area_labels() {
  local concerns_path="$target_root/docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md"

  if [[ -s "$concerns_path" ]]; then
    awk '
      match($0, /`area:[^`]+`/) {
        label=substr($0, RSTART + 1, RLENGTH - 2)
        if (!seen[label]++) print label
      }
    ' "$concerns_path"
    return 0
  fi

  printf '%s\n' \
    'area:ui' \
    'area:api' \
    'area:data' \
    'area:infra' \
    'area:cli'
}

resolve_alignment_scope_path() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local helper="${library_root}/scripts/refresh_context_digests.py"

  [[ "$scope" == "repo" || "$scope" == area:* ]] && return 0
  [[ -f "$helper" ]] || return 0
  command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0

  python3 - "$target_root" "$library_root" "$scope" <<'PY'
from importlib.util import module_from_spec, spec_from_file_location
from pathlib import Path
import sys

root = Path(sys.argv[1])
library_root = Path(sys.argv[2])
scope = sys.argv[3]
helper = library_root / "scripts" / "refresh_context_digests.py"

spec = spec_from_file_location("refresh_context_digests", helper)
if spec is None or spec.loader is None:
    raise SystemExit(0)
module = module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(module)
path = module.resolve_artifact_path(root, scope, library_root)
if path is None:
    raise SystemExit(0)
for base in (root, library_root):
    try:
        print(path.relative_to(base).as_posix())
        break
    except ValueError:
        continue
PY
}

alignment_scope_area_labels() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local resolved_path=""

  if [[ "$scope" == "repo" ]]; then
    alignment_project_area_labels | awk 'NF && !seen[$0]++'
    return 0
  fi

  {
    [[ "$scope" == area:* ]] && printf '%s\n' "$scope"
    alignment_area_labels_from_path "$scope"
    resolved_path="$(resolve_alignment_scope_path "$scope" || true)"
    if [[ -n "$resolved_path" ]]; then
      alignment_area_labels_from_path "$resolved_path"
    fi
  } | awk 'NF && !seen[$0]++'
}

alignment_scope_areas_text() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local label="" joined=""

  while IFS= read -r label; do
    [[ -n "$label" ]] || continue
    if [[ -n "$joined" ]]; then
      joined+=", "
    fi
    joined+="${label#area:}"
  done < <(alignment_scope_area_labels "$scope")

  printf '%s\n' "$joined"
}

alignment_bead_labels() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local labels='helix,phase:review,kind:planning,action:align'
  local label=""

  while IFS= read -r label; do
    [[ -n "$label" ]] || continue
    labels+=",${label}"
  done < <(alignment_scope_area_labels "$scope")

  printf '%s\n' "$labels"
}

merge_alignment_bead_labels() {
  local bead_id="$1" scope="${2:-repo}"
  local issue_json="" current_csv="" merged_csv=""

  issue_json="$(tracker_show "$bead_id" --json 2>/dev/null)" || return 1
  current_csv="$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -r '(.labels // []) | join(",")' 2>/dev/null)" || return 1
  merged_csv="$(
    {
      printf '%s\n' "$current_csv" | tr ',' '\n'
      alignment_bead_labels "$scope" | tr ',' '\n'
    } | awk '
      NF && !seen[$0]++ {
        labels[++count] = $0
      }
      END {
        for (i = 1; i <= count; i++) {
          printf "%s%s", labels[i], (i < count ? "," : ORS)
        }
      }
    '
  )" || return 1

  [[ "$merged_csv" == "$current_csv" ]] && return 0
  tracker_update "$bead_id" --labels "$merged_csv" >/dev/null || return 1
}

alignment_bead_description() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local scope_areas=""
  scope_areas="$(alignment_scope_areas_text "$scope")"
  cat <<EOF
Top-down reconciliation review for ${scope}.
Scope: ${scope}.
${scope_areas:+Scope areas: ${scope_areas}.}
EOF
}

infer_alignment_spec_id() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  case "$scope" in
    FEAT-*|US-*|SD-*|TD-*|TP-*|ADR-*|CONTRACT-*)
      printf '%s\n' "$scope"
      ;;
  esac
}

find_alignment_bead() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}" status="${2:-open}"
  local title
  title="$(alignment_bead_title "$scope")"
  tracker_list --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r --arg title "$title" --arg status "$status" '
    [.[] | select(
      (.status // "") == $status and
      ((.labels // []) | index("kind:planning")) and
      ((.labels // []) | index("action:align")) and
      (.title // "") == $title
    )][0].id // empty
  '
}

claim_reclaim_status_reason=""
claim_reclaim_status_pid=""
claim_reclaim_status_age=0

issue_claim_reclaimable() {
  local issue_json="$1"
  local process_pattern="${2:-}"
  local stale_threshold="${HELIX_ORPHAN_THRESHOLD:-7200}"  # default 2 hours
  local claimed_at claimed_pid updated claim_epoch=0 now_epoch age=0

  claim_reclaim_status_reason=""
  claim_reclaim_status_pid="unknown"
  claim_reclaim_status_age=0

  claimed_at="$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -r '."claimed-at" // ""' 2>/dev/null)"
  claimed_pid="$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -r '."claimed-pid" // ""' 2>/dev/null)"
  updated="$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -r '.updated_at // ""' 2>/dev/null)"

  [[ "$claimed_pid" == "null" ]] && claimed_pid=""
  claim_reclaim_status_pid="${claimed_pid:-unknown}"

  if [[ -n "$process_pattern" ]] && pgrep -f "$process_pattern" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    claim_reclaim_status_reason="busy"
    return 1
  fi

  if [[ -n "$claimed_pid" ]] && kill -0 "$claimed_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
    claim_reclaim_status_reason="pid_alive"
    return 1
  fi

  if [[ -n "$claimed_at" && "$claimed_at" != "null" ]]; then
    claim_epoch="$(date -d "$claimed_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  elif [[ -n "$updated" && "$updated" != "null" ]]; then
    claim_epoch="$(date -d "$updated" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  fi

  now_epoch="$(date +%s)"
  if (( claim_epoch > 0 )); then
    age=$(( now_epoch - claim_epoch ))
  fi
  claim_reclaim_status_age="$age"

  if (( claim_epoch > 0 && age < stale_threshold )); then
    claim_reclaim_status_reason="fresh"
    return 1
  fi

  claim_reclaim_status_reason="stale"
  return 0
}

ensure_alignment_bead() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local existing_id active_id spec_id new_id labels

  existing_id="$(find_alignment_bead "$scope")" || return 1
  if [[ -n "$existing_id" ]]; then
    tracker_update "$existing_id" --claim >/dev/null || return 1
    merge_alignment_bead_labels "$existing_id" "$scope" || return 1
    refresh_context_digest_for_bead "$existing_id"
    printf '%s\t%s\n' "$existing_id" "claimed"
    return 0
  fi

  active_id="$(find_alignment_bead "$scope" "in_progress")" || return 1
  if [[ -n "$active_id" ]]; then
    local active_owner active_json active_status live_active_json live_active_status
    active_json="$(tracker_show "$active_id" --json 2>/dev/null || true)"
    active_status="$(printf '%s' "$active_json" | ddx jq -r '.status // ""' 2>/dev/null)" || active_status=""
    if [[ "$active_status" != "in_progress" ]]; then
      ensure_alignment_bead "$scope"
      return $?
    fi
    if issue_claim_reclaimable "$active_json"; then
      live_active_json="$(tracker_show "$active_id" --json 2>/dev/null || true)"
      live_active_status="$(printf '%s' "$live_active_json" | ddx jq -r '.status // ""' 2>/dev/null)" || live_active_status=""
      if [[ "$live_active_status" != "in_progress" ]]; then
        verbose_line "alignment bead %s for %s changed to %s before reclaim; skipping duplicate dispatch" \
          "$active_id" "$scope" "${live_active_status:-missing}"
        return 1
      fi
      if ! issue_claim_reclaimable "$live_active_json"; then
        verbose_line "alignment bead %s for %s is no longer reclaimable (%s); skipping duplicate dispatch" \
          "$active_id" "$scope" "${claim_reclaim_status_reason:-unknown}"
        return 1
      fi
      verbose_line "alignment bead %s for %s is orphaned (age=%ss, pid=%s); reclaiming" \
        "$active_id" "$scope" "$claim_reclaim_status_age" "$claim_reclaim_status_pid"
      tracker_update "$active_id" --unclaim >/dev/null || return 1
      tracker_update "$active_id" --claim >/dev/null || return 1
      merge_alignment_bead_labels "$active_id" "$scope" || return 1
      refresh_context_digest_for_bead "$active_id"
      printf '%s\t%s\n' "$active_id" "reclaimed"
      return 0
    fi
    active_owner="$(printf '%s' "$active_json" | ddx jq -r '.owner // ""' 2>/dev/null)" || active_owner=""
    if [[ -n "$active_owner" ]]; then
      summary_line "alignment already active for %s under owner %s (%s); refusing duplicate dispatch" "$scope" "$active_owner" "$active_id"
    else
      summary_line "alignment already active for %s (%s); refusing duplicate dispatch" "$scope" "$active_id"
    fi
    return 1
  fi

  spec_id="$(infer_alignment_spec_id "$scope" || true)"
  labels="$(alignment_bead_labels "$scope")"
  if [[ -n "$spec_id" ]]; then
    new_id="$(tracker_create \
      "$(alignment_bead_title "$scope")" \
      --type task \
      --labels "$labels" \
      --spec-id "$spec_id" \
      --description "$(alignment_bead_description "$scope")" \
      --acceptance "$(alignment_bead_acceptance)")" || return 1
  else
    new_id="$(tracker_create \
      "$(alignment_bead_title "$scope")" \
      --type task \
      --labels "$labels" \
      --description "$(alignment_bead_description "$scope")" \
      --acceptance "$(alignment_bead_acceptance)")" || return 1
  fi

  refresh_context_digest_for_bead "$new_id"
  printf '%s\t%s\n' "$new_id" "created"
}

# Unclaim an issue only if it is still in-progress.
# Never unclaims a closed bead — semantically wrong and kept as a guard;
# ddx bead update --unclaim only clears claim metadata on closed beads
# without changing their status (does not reopen them).
safe_unclaim() {
  local issue_id="$1"
  local current_status
  current_status="$(tracker_show "$issue_id" --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status // ""')" || return 0
  if [[ "$current_status" == "closed" ]]; then
    verbose_line "safe_unclaim: %s is already closed — skipping unclaim to prevent state revert" "$issue_id"
    return 0
  fi
  tracker_update "$issue_id" --unclaim 2>/dev/null || true
}

tracker_list() {
  tracker_exec list "$@"
}

tracker_ready() {
  tracker_exec ready "$@"
}

tracker_blocked() {
  tracker_exec blocked "$@"
}

tracker_dep() {
  local subcmd="$1"
  shift

  case "$subcmd" in
    add|remove|tree) tracker_exec dep "$subcmd" "$@" ;;
    *) echo "tracker: dep $subcmd: unknown subcommand" >&2; return 1 ;;
  esac
}

tracker_status() {
  tracker_exec status "$@"
}

tracker_import() {
  tracker_exec import "$@"
}

tracker_export() {
  tracker_exec export "$@"
}

# ---------- PID file helpers (hx-407ed8b8) ----------

_helix_pid_file() {
  printf '%s/helix.pid' "$tracker_dir"
}

_helix_pid_alive() {
  local pid_file
  pid_file="$(_helix_pid_file)"
  [[ -f "$pid_file" ]] || return 1
  local pid
  pid="$(cat "$pid_file" 2>/dev/null)"
  [[ -n "$pid" ]] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null
}

run_start() {
  require_tracker
  local pid_file
  pid_file="$(_helix_pid_file)"

  if _helix_pid_alive; then
    local pid; pid="$(cat "$pid_file")"
    summary_line "run already active (pid=%s). Use 'helix stop' first." "$pid"
    return 1
  fi

  # Clean stale PID file
  rm -f "$pid_file"

  # Launch helix run in background with --summary
  "$0" run --summary "$@" &
  local bg_pid=$!
  printf '%s\n' "$bg_pid" > "$pid_file"
  summary_line "started (pid=%s, log=%s)" "$bg_pid" "$helix_log"
}

run_stop() {
  require_tracker
  local pid_file
  pid_file="$(_helix_pid_file)"

  if [[ ! -f "$pid_file" ]]; then
    summary_line "no active run (no PID file)"
    return 1
  fi

  local pid; pid="$(cat "$pid_file" 2>/dev/null)"

  if ! kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
    rm -f "$pid_file"
    summary_line "stale PID file removed (pid=%s was not running)" "$pid"
    return 1
  fi

  kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null
  local waited=0
  while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && (( waited < 10 )); do
    sleep 1
    (( waited++ ))
  done

  if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
    kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  rm -f "$pid_file"
  printf 'helix: stopped (pid=%s)\n' "$pid" >&2
}

# ---------- DDx exec run integration (FEAT-005) ----------
#
# When ddx exec is available, helix CLI dispatch wraps skill invocations
# with ddx exec run for observability. Run records persist after each
# skill invocation.
#
# Recursion protection: when scripts/helix is invoked BY ddx exec run,
# the env var HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 is set. In that case the exec wrap
# is skipped and the skill executes directly.
#
# Pattern:
#   helix build
#     → scripts/helix main() detects exec available, HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT unset
#     → HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 ddx exec run helix:build
#       → ddx exec run invokes: helix build (with HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1)
#       → scripts/helix main() sees HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 → skips exec wrap
#       → executes run_implementation directly
#       → ddx records the run
#
# All skill command arguments are preserved because ddx exec run re-invokes
# the full helix CLI command from the exec definition.

_ddx_exec_available() {
  command -v ddx &>/dev/null || return 1
  ddx exec list &>/dev/null 2>&1
}

# Returns 0 if we should wrap this dispatch in ddx exec run.
_exec_wrap_enabled() {
  local skill="$1"
  [[ "${HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT:-0}" == "1" ]] && return 1  # already inside exec run
  (( dry_run )) && return 1                               # dry-run: skip
  _ddx_exec_available || return 1                         # ddx exec not available
  ddx exec validate "helix:${skill}" &>/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1  # definition missing
  return 0
}

# exec_wrap <skill> <helix-args...>
# Wraps a top-level skill dispatch with ddx exec run when available.
# On exec wrap: re-invokes helix with HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1.
# On fallback: calls "$@" (the original function + args) directly.
exec_wrap() {
  local skill="$1"; shift
  if _exec_wrap_enabled "$skill"; then
    verbose_line "wrapping helix:%s with ddx exec run" "$skill"
    HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 ddx exec run "helix:${skill}"
  else
    "$@"
  fi
}

ddx_managed_execution_available() {
  command -v ddx >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
  ddx agent execute-bead --help >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
  ddx agent execute-loop --help >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
  return 0
}

managed_build_agent() {
  local exec_agent="$agent"
  if [[ -n "$alt_agent" ]] && [[ "$alt_agent" != "$agent" ]]; then
    exec_agent="$alt_agent"
  fi
  printf '%s' "$exec_agent"
}

managed_build_model_override() {
  local exec_agent="${1:-}"

  # Keep an explicit model override only when managed execution stays on the
  # configured primary harness. This avoids leaking codex/claude-specific
  # concrete model names across adversarial rotation boundaries.
  if [[ -n "${agent_model:-}" ]] && [[ -z "$exec_agent" || "$exec_agent" == "$agent" ]]; then
    printf '%s' "$agent_model"
  fi
}

resolve_execution_target() {
  local selector="${1:-}"

  tracker_ready --json --execution | ddx jq -r --arg selector "$selector" '
    if $selector == "" then
      .[0].id // empty
    else
      [
        .[] |
        select(
          .id == $selector or
          (.parent // "") == $selector or
          (.["spec-id"] // "") == $selector or
          ((.labels // []) | index($selector)) or
          ((.title // "") | contains($selector))
        )
      ][0].id // empty
    end
  '
}

run_execute_bead() {
  local issue_id="${1:-}"
  local exec_agent ddx_harness model_override effort_override
  local -a ddx_flags=()

  exec_agent="$(managed_build_agent)"
  ddx_harness="$(_resolve_ddx_dispatch_harness "$exec_agent")"
  model_override="$(managed_build_model_override "$exec_agent")"
  effort_override="${agent_effort:-medium}"

  [[ -n "$ddx_harness" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--harness "$ddx_harness")
  [[ -n "$model_override" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--model "$model_override")
  [[ -n "$effort_override" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--effort "$effort_override")

  if (( dry_run )); then
    printf 'ddx agent execute-bead %s' "${issue_id:-<ready-bead>}"
    local flag
    for flag in "${ddx_flags[@]}"; do
      printf ' %q' "$flag"
    done
    printf '\n'
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ -z "$issue_id" ]]; then
    summary_line "no execution-ready bead matched the build selector"
    return 1
  fi

  verbose_line "delegating helix build to ddx agent execute-bead for %s" "$issue_id"
  env HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE="$issue_id" ddx agent execute-bead "$issue_id" "${ddx_flags[@]}"
}

run_execute_loop_once() {
  local selected_issue="${1:-}"
  local exec_agent ddx_harness model_override effort_override
  local -a ddx_flags=(--once)

  exec_agent="$(managed_build_agent)"
  ddx_harness="$(_resolve_ddx_dispatch_harness "$exec_agent")"
  model_override="$(managed_build_model_override "$exec_agent")"
  effort_override="${agent_effort:-medium}"

  [[ -n "$ddx_harness" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--harness "$ddx_harness")
  [[ -n "$model_override" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--model "$model_override")
  [[ -n "$effort_override" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--effort "$effort_override")

  if (( dry_run )); then
    printf 'ddx agent execute-loop'
    local flag
    for flag in "${ddx_flags[@]}"; do
      printf ' %q' "$flag"
    done
    printf '\n'
    return 0
  fi

  verbose_line "delegating helix run build cycle to ddx agent execute-loop --once"
  env HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE="$selected_issue" ddx agent execute-loop "${ddx_flags[@]}"
}

tracker_dispatch() {
  local subcmd="${1:-}"
  require_tracker
  shift || true

  case "$subcmd" in
    help|-h|--help|"") tracker_exec --help ;;
    create) tracker_create "$@" ;;
    update) tracker_update "$@" ;;
    import) tracker_import "$@" ;;
    export) tracker_export "$@" ;;
    *) tracker_exec "$subcmd" "$@" ;;
  esac
}

extract_codex_tokens() {
  # Extract the token count from codex output (appears as "tokens used\nNNN").
  # Prints the count or 0 if not found.
  local prev=""
  while IFS= read -r line; do
    if [[ "$prev" == "tokens used" ]] && [[ "$line" =~ ^[0-9,]+$ ]]; then
      printf '%s' "${line//,/}"
      return
    fi
    prev="$line"
  done
  printf '0'
}

reset_session() {
  session_active=0
  if (( ! quiet )); then
    printf 'helix: session reset (next cycle starts fresh)\n' >&2
  fi
}

run_agent_prompt() {
  local prompt="$1"
  # Only apply agent_model when no explicit override is given AND
  # the current agent matches the configured primary agent.
  # This prevents passing claude models to codex during cross-model review.
  local model_override="${2:-}"
  if [[ -z "$model_override" ]] && [[ "$agent" == "$(normalize_agent_name "${HELIX_AGENT:-codex}")" ]]; then
    model_override="$agent_model"
  fi
  local effort_override="${3:-$agent_effort}"
  local prompt_bytes
  prompt_bytes="$(printf '%s' "$prompt" | wc -c)"
  local agent_timeout="${HELIX_AGENT_TIMEOUT:-2700}"

  # Prefer ddx agent run for unified dispatch (token tracking, session logging,
  # harness-agnostic interface). Fall back to direct dispatch only when ddx
  # agent is unavailable. Set HELIX_DIRECT_AGENT=1 to skip ddx and dispatch
  # to agent binaries directly (used by tests with mock agents).
  if [[ "${HELIX_DIRECT_AGENT:-0}" != "1" ]] \
     && command -v ddx &>/dev/null \
     && ddx agent list &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    _run_agent_ddx "$prompt" "$model_override" "$effort_override" "$agent_timeout"
    return $?
  fi

  # Fallback: direct agent dispatch (no ddx)
  _run_agent_direct "$prompt" "$model_override" "$effort_override" "$agent_timeout"
}

# Extract agent text result from ddx agent run JSON output.
# Claude: output is a JSON blob with .result field.
# Codex: output is JSONL stream; text is in item.completed .item.text fields.
# Other: emit output as-is.
_extract_agent_text() {
  local harness="$1" raw_output="$2"

  case "$harness" in
    claude)
      # Claude output is a JSON result blob — extract .result
      printf '%s' "$raw_output" | ddx jq -r '.result // ""' 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "$raw_output"
      ;;
    codex)
      # Codex output is JSONL — extract text from item.completed events
      printf '%s\n' "$raw_output" | while IFS= read -r line; do
        local text
        text="$(printf '%s' "$line" | ddx jq -r 'select(.type == "item.completed") | .item.text // empty' 2>/dev/null || true)"
        [[ -n "$text" ]] && printf '%s\n' "$text"
      done
      ;;
    *)
      printf '%s' "$raw_output"
      ;;
  esac
}

# Primary dispatch via ddx agent run.
_run_agent_ddx() {
  local prompt="$1" model_override="$2" effort_override="$3" agent_timeout="$4"
  local ddx_harness
  ddx_harness="$(_resolve_ddx_dispatch_harness "$agent")"
  local prompt_bytes
  prompt_bytes="$(printf '%s' "$prompt" | wc -c)"

    --harness "$ddx_harness"

  [[ -n "$effort_override" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--effort "$effort_override")

      "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" "$ddx_harness" "${agent:+ (requested: $agent)}" "${model_override:+(model: $model_override)}" "$prompt_bytes" "$agent_timeout" >&2
  fi

  # Dispatch via ddx agent run with JSON output for metrics
  local _agent_tmp _agent_rc=0
  _agent_tmp="$(mktemp)"
  ddx agent run "${ddx_flags[@]}" --json --prompt "$_prompt_tmp" > "$_agent_tmp" 2>&1 &
  local _agent_pid=$!

  # Watchdog: periodic heartbeat while ddx handles its own timeout
  local _watchdog_elapsed=0
  while kill -0 "$_agent_pid" 2>/dev/null; do
    sleep 5
    _watchdog_elapsed=$((_watchdog_elapsed + 5))
    if (( ! quiet )) && (( _watchdog_elapsed % 60 == 0 )); then
      printf 'helix: [%s] %s still running (%ss elapsed)\n' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" "$ddx_harness" "$_watchdog_elapsed" >&2
    fi

  wait "$_agent_pid" || _agent_rc=$?

  rm -f "$_prompt_tmp"

  local _agent_json
  _agent_json="$(cat "$_agent_tmp")"
  rm -f "$_agent_tmp"

  local _elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - _start_ts ))

  # Extract metrics from ddx JSON output
  local _cycle_tokens _cost _exit_code _raw_output
  _exit_code="$(printf '%s' "$_agent_json" | ddx jq -r '.exit_code // 1' 2>/dev/null || echo 1)"
  _cycle_tokens="$(printf '%s' "$_agent_json" | ddx jq -r '(.input_tokens // 0) + (.output_tokens // 0)' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  _cost="$(printf '%s' "$_agent_json" | ddx jq -r '.cost_usd // 0' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"

alignment_scope_area_labels() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local resolved_path=""

  if [[ "$scope" == "repo" ]]; then
    alignment_project_area_labels | awk 'NF && !seen[$0]++'
    return 0
  fi

  {
    [[ "$scope" == area:* ]] && printf '%s\n' "$scope"
    alignment_area_labels_from_path "$scope"
    resolved_path="$(resolve_alignment_scope_path "$scope" || true)"
    if [[ -n "$resolved_path" ]]; then
      alignment_area_labels_from_path "$resolved_path"
    fi
  } | awk 'NF && !seen[$0]++'
}

alignment_scope_areas_text() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local label="" joined=""

  while IFS= read -r label; do
    [[ -n "$label" ]] || continue
    if [[ -n "$joined" ]]; then
      joined+=", "
    fi
    joined+="${label#area:}"
  done < <(alignment_scope_area_labels "$scope")

  printf '%s\n' "$joined"
}

alignment_bead_labels() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local labels='helix,phase:review,kind:planning,action:align'
  local label=""

  while IFS= read -r label; do
    [[ -n "$label" ]] || continue
    labels+=",${label}"
  done < <(alignment_scope_area_labels "$scope")

  printf '%s\n' "$labels"
}

merge_alignment_bead_labels() {
  local bead_id="$1" scope="${2:-repo}"
  local issue_json="" current_csv="" merged_csv=""

  issue_json="$(tracker_show "$bead_id" --json 2>/dev/null)" || return 1
  current_csv="$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -r '(.labels // []) | join(",")' 2>/dev/null)" || return 1
  merged_csv="$(
    {
      printf '%s\n' "$current_csv" | tr ',' '\n'
      alignment_bead_labels "$scope" | tr ',' '\n'
    } | awk '
      NF && !seen[$0]++ {
        labels[++count] = $0
      }
      END {
        for (i = 1; i <= count; i++) {
          printf "%s%s", labels[i], (i < count ? "," : ORS)
        }
      }
    '
  )" || return 1

  [[ "$merged_csv" == "$current_csv" ]] && return 0
  tracker_update "$bead_id" --labels "$merged_csv" >/dev/null || return 1
}

alignment_bead_description() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local scope_areas=""
  scope_areas="$(alignment_scope_areas_text "$scope")"
  cat <<EOF
Top-down reconciliation review for ${scope}.
Scope: ${scope}.
${scope_areas:+Scope areas: ${scope_areas}.}
EOF
}

infer_alignment_spec_id() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  case "$scope" in
    FEAT-*|US-*|SD-*|TD-*|TP-*|ADR-*|CONTRACT-*)
      printf '%s\n' "$scope"
      ;;
  esac
}

find_alignment_bead() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}" status="${2:-open}"
  local title
  title="$(alignment_bead_title "$scope")"
  tracker_list --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r --arg title "$title" --arg status "$status" '
    [.[] | select(
      (.status // "") == $status and
      ((.labels // []) | index("kind:planning")) and
      ((.labels // []) | index("action:align")) and
      (.title // "") == $title
    )][0].id // empty
  '
}

claim_reclaim_status_reason=""
claim_reclaim_status_pid=""
claim_reclaim_status_age=0

issue_claim_reclaimable() {
  local issue_json="$1"
  local process_pattern="${2:-}"
  local stale_threshold="${HELIX_ORPHAN_THRESHOLD:-7200}"  # default 2 hours
  local claimed_at claimed_pid updated claim_epoch=0 now_epoch age=0

  claim_reclaim_status_reason=""
  claim_reclaim_status_pid="unknown"
  claim_reclaim_status_age=0

  claimed_at="$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -r '."claimed-at" // ""' 2>/dev/null)"
  claimed_pid="$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -r '."claimed-pid" // ""' 2>/dev/null)"
  updated="$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | ddx jq -r '.updated_at // ""' 2>/dev/null)"

  [[ "$claimed_pid" == "null" ]] && claimed_pid=""
  claim_reclaim_status_pid="${claimed_pid:-unknown}"

  if [[ -n "$process_pattern" ]] && pgrep -f "$process_pattern" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    claim_reclaim_status_reason="busy"
    return 1
  fi

  if [[ -n "$claimed_pid" ]] && kill -0 "$claimed_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
    claim_reclaim_status_reason="pid_alive"
    return 1
  fi

  if [[ -n "$claimed_at" && "$claimed_at" != "null" ]]; then
    claim_epoch="$(date -d "$claimed_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  elif [[ -n "$updated" && "$updated" != "null" ]]; then
    claim_epoch="$(date -d "$updated" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  fi

  now_epoch="$(date +%s)"
  if (( claim_epoch > 0 )); then
    age=$(( now_epoch - claim_epoch ))
  fi
  claim_reclaim_status_age="$age"

  if (( claim_epoch > 0 && age < stale_threshold )); then
    claim_reclaim_status_reason="fresh"
    return 1
  fi

  claim_reclaim_status_reason="stale"
  return 0
}

ensure_alignment_bead() {
  local scope="${1:-repo}"
  local existing_id active_id spec_id new_id labels

  existing_id="$(find_alignment_bead "$scope")" || return 1
  if [[ -n "$existing_id" ]]; then

    merge_alignment_bead_labels "$existing_id" "$scope" || return 1
    refresh_context_digest_for_bead "$existing_id"
    printf '%s\t%s\n' "$existing_id" "claimed"
    return 0

.ddx/executions/20260417T215238-6f0001e4/prompt.md:5:Create tests/helix-loop-integration.sh (or equivalent under tests/) that drives real scripts/helix against a stubbed ddx emitting scripted execute-loop --json output. Coverage requirements:
(a) no_ready_work: helix run exits cleanly with no cycle counted
(b) single success: one results[] entry with status=success, HELIX applies post-cycle to that bead
(c) mixed success/failure in one drain
(d) retry-after cooldown: results[] entry with retry_after set; HELIX does not unclaim or blame
(e) preserve_ref present: HELIX surfaces it without attempting land
(f) tolerant parsing: unknown top-level or per-result fields MUST be ignored without error
(g) strict required fields: missing bead_id or status MUST cause a clear structured error, not a silent pass

CI exercise of the contract matrix produced by the audit bead: each DDx-owned item with a code-link resolution has a harness test asserting the linked behavior; each new-DDx-bead resolution is tracked as skipped-pending; each HELIX-backport resolution has an in-harness fake verifying the HELIX fallback.

## Manual Loop

This is the canonical operator path once work is execution-ready:

```bash
while [ "$(helix_ready_count)" -gt 0 ]; do
  ddx agent execute-loop --once
done

helix check
```

`helix run` and `helix build` may still be used as compatibility wrappers when
an operator wants HELIX to provide transitional routing or wrapper ergonomics,
but the durable queue-drain primitive is `ddx agent execute-loop`, not an
independent HELIX-owned claim/execute/close loop.

### Architecture

HELIX owns **queue curation**: maintaining accurate bead topology (dependencies,
`execution-eligible`, `superseded-by`, epic hierarchy) so DDx's deterministic
`ReadyExecution()` ordering produces the intended sequence. HELIX does not
predict which bead DDx will select.

DDx owns **loop, selection, and execution**: bead selection, managed worktree
execution, close-with-evidence, retry suppression, and orphan recovery.

The intended adoption end state is: after each
`ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` call, HELIX parses `results[].bead_id`
and `results[].status`, then applies post-cycle supervisory policy to the bead
DDx actually executed, not a pre-selected bead.

### Current compatibility behavior

- `helix run` keeps HELIX-owned supervisory routing, queue-health decisions,
  post-build review/alignment hooks, and persisted run state, while delegating
  each build cycle to `ddx agent execute-loop --once`.
- `helix build` delegates the managed execution attempt to
  `ddx agent execute-bead` for explicit single-bead execution.
- Epic focus: HELIX sets `execution-eligible: false` on non-child beads to bias
  DDx's deterministic ordering toward epic children; no DDx flag needed.
- Current gap: the wrapper still pre-selects a bead and exports
  `HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE` before DDx chooses the actual execution target. That is
  a temporary internal seam, not part of the HELIX/DDx contract.
- Current gap: review and periodic alignment still run as wrapper-owned
  post-cycle hooks. The queued-bead model is the target state, but it is not
  yet the shipped wrapper behavior.
- Current gap: the wrapper still carries legacy retry/backoff, skip-tracking,
  and blocker-report logic. The durable contract remains DDx-owned cooldown via
  `execute-loop-retry-after` plus tracker visibility through `ddx bead blocked`.

Interpret `check` as follows:

- `NEXT_ACTION: BUILD`
  More safe ready work exists; continue.
- `NEXT_ACTION: DESIGN`
  Run `helix design <scope>` once, then re-run `check`.
- `NEXT_ACTION: POLISH`
  Run `helix polish <scope>` once to decompose plans and refine issues, then
  re-run `check`.
- `NEXT_ACTION: ALIGN`
  Run `reconcile-alignment` once for the indicated scope if auto-alignment is
  enabled, then re-run `check`.
- `NEXT_ACTION: BACKFILL`
  Stop and hand off to `backfill-helix-docs` for the indicated scope.
- `NEXT_ACTION: WAIT`
  Stop. Do not attempt to build around the blocker or auto-unblock it.
- `NEXT_ACTION: GUIDANCE`
  Stop and get user or stakeholder input.
- `NEXT_ACTION: STOP`
  No actionable work remains for the current scope.

`helix run` is a bounded compatibility controller, not a repair loop.

- It counts only completed build passes toward `--max-cycles`.
- Contract target: parse `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`, use
  `results[].bead_id` for all post-cycle bookkeeping, and avoid any private
  selector handoff that DDx does not expose.
- It may dispatch `helix design` or `helix polish` before build when
  supervisory state indicates missing design authority, undecomposed plans,
  or stale issue refinement.
- It may run fresh-eyes review after a successful build when review
  automation is enabled; `--no-auto-review` disables that post-implementation
  review.
- It may switch to `--review-agent` for cross-model verification during review.
- It may run `reconcile-alignment` every `N` completed implementation passes
  when `--review-every N` is set; `--no-auto-align` disables that post-drain
  alignment step.
- It may persist run-controller state for `helix status` including focused epic,
  attempt counters, cycle timing, and token totals.
- It should refresh `.helix/context.md` at run start, on epic switch, and
  every 5 completed implementation passes so long-lived sessions keep current
  build/test commands and tracker counts in view.
- It may stay on a focused epic until all children are done, then run a scoped
  post-epic review before leaving that scope. Epic focus uses `execution-eligible`
  curation, not DDx selection flags.
- Queue drift (superseded-by, parent change, spec-id change) is caught at `helix check`

run_execute_loop_once() {
  local selected_issue="${1:-}"
  local exec_agent ddx_harness model_override effort_override
  local -a ddx_flags=(--once)

  exec_agent="$(managed_build_agent)"
  ddx_harness="$(_resolve_ddx_dispatch_harness "$exec_agent")"
  model_override="$(managed_build_model_override "$exec_agent")"
  effort_override="${agent_effort:-medium}"

  [[ -n "$ddx_harness" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--harness "$ddx_harness")
  [[ -n "$model_override" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--model "$model_override")
  [[ -n "$effort_override" ]] && ddx_flags+=(--effort "$effort_override")

  if (( dry_run )); then
    printf 'ddx agent execute-loop'
    local flag
    for flag in "${ddx_flags[@]}"; do
      printf ' %q' "$flag"
    done
    printf '\n'
    return 0
  fi

  verbose_line "delegating helix run build cycle to ddx agent execute-loop --once"
  env HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE="$selected_issue" ddx agent execute-loop "${ddx_flags[@]}"
}

tracker_dispatch() {
  local subcmd="${1:-}"
  require_tracker
  shift || true

  case "$subcmd" in
    help|-h|--help|"") tracker_exec --help ;;
    create) tracker_create "$@" ;;
    update) tracker_update "$@" ;;
    import) tracker_import "$@" ;;
    export) tracker_export "$@" ;;
    *) tracker_exec "$subcmd" "$@" ;;
  esac
}

extract_codex_tokens() {
  # Extract the token count from codex output (appears as "tokens used\nNNN").
  # Prints the count or 0 if not found.
  local prev=""
  while IFS= read -r line; do
    if [[ "$prev" == "tokens used" ]] && [[ "$line" =~ ^[0-9,]+$ ]]; then
      printf '%s' "${line//,/}"
      return
    fi
    prev="$line"
  done
  printf '0'
}

reset_session() {
  session_active=0
  if (( ! quiet )); then
    printf 'helix: session reset (next cycle starts fresh)\n' >&2
  fi
}

  local close_after_show_count="\${MOCK_CLOSE_AFTER_SHOW_COUNT:-1}"

pick_issue() {

    printf '%s\n' "\$HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE"

}

dispatch_managed_execution() {

      ;;
    *)
      codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox \
        "implementation action" "Implementation target: \$issue_id"
      ;;
  esac
}

test_run_delegates_build_cycles_to_execute_loop() {

  root="$(make_workspace)"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
state_root="${MOCK_STATE_ROOT:?}"

next_action() {

# =============================================================================
# helix-loop-integration.sh
# =============================================================================
# Integration tests for the execute-loop --json response contract.
#
# Drives real scripts/helix against a stubbed ddx that emits scripted
# execute-loop --json output.  Each fixture covers one DDx --json response
# shape and asserts the expected HELIX side-effect (post-cycle bookkeeping,
# error output, exit code).
#
# Contract rows (DDx-owned, HELIX consumers):
#   (a) no_ready_work        — processed=0, results=[] → clean exit, 0 cycles
#   (b) single success       — results[].status=success → post-cycle close
#   (c) mixed success/fail   — results[].status=success|failure → per-bead post-cycle
#   (d) retry_after cooldown — results[].retry_after set → helix does NOT unclaim/blame
#   (e) preserve_ref         — results[].preserve_ref present → surfaced, not landed
#   (f) tolerant parsing     — unknown fields ignored silently (top-level + per-result)
#   (g) strict required fields — missing bead_id OR missing status → structured error
#
# The contract matrix (CONTRACT-001-audit.md) links each DDx-owned item to a
# code-link.  Each code-link row gets an in-harness test asserting the linked
# behavior.  Each new-DDx-bead row is tracked as skipped-pending.
# Each HELIX-backport row has an in-harness fake verifying the HELIX fallback.
# =============================================================================
set -euo pipefail

export DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK=1

# ── Helper borrowed from helix-cli.sh ──────────────────────────────

repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"

# Defensive ddx wrapper: strip advisory lines before they reach JSON consumers.
ddx() {
  local _ddx_out _ddx_rc=0
  _ddx_out="$(command ddx "$@")" || _ddx_rc=$?
  printf '%s\n' "$_ddx_out" | grep -v '^💡\|^⬆️' || true
  return "$_ddx_rc"
}
export -f ddx

test_count=0

fail() {
  echo "FAIL: $*" >&2
  exit 1
}

assert_eq() {
  local expected="$1" actual="$2" msg="$3"
  if [[ "$expected" != "$actual" ]]; then
    printf 'expected:\n%s\nactual:\n%s\n' "$expected" "$actual" >&2
    fail "$msg"
  fi
}

assert_contains() {
  local haystack="$1" needle="$2" msg="$3"
  if [[ "$haystack" != *"$needle"* ]]; then
    printf 'missing substring: %s\nin:\n%s\n' "$needle" "$haystack" >&2
    fail "$msg"
  fi
}

assert_not_contains() {
  local haystack="$1" needle="$2" msg="$3"
  if [[ "$haystack" == *"$needle"* ]]; then
    printf 'unexpected substring: %s\nin:\n%s\n' "$needle" "$haystack" >&2
    fail "$msg"
  fi
}

set -euo pipefail

real_ddx="__REAL_DDX__"
state_root="${MOCK_STATE_ROOT:?}"

record_execute() {
  printf '%s\n' "$1" >> "$state_root/ddx-calls.log"
}

pick_issue() {
  if [[ -n "${HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE:-}" ]] \
     && "$real_ddx" bead show "$HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE" --json >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf '%s\n' "$HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE"
    return 0
  fi

    | "$real_ddx" jq -r '.[0].id // empty'
}

dispatch_managed_execution() {
  local harness="$1" issue_id="$2"
  # For integration tests: simulate a zero-delayed agent that always succeeds.
  # The mock always "succeeds" — the execute-loop JSON response drives assertions.

# Load scripted execute-loop response from state file if present.
# Falls back to compatibility mode (pick_issue + dispatch).
handle_execute_loop() {
  shift 2  # drop "agent execute-loop"
  local harness="codex" json_mode=0
  local -a remaining=()

  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --harness) harness="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      --json)   json_mode=1;  shift ;;
      --once|--model|--effort|--from|--poll-interval) shift ;;
      *)        remaining+=("$1"); shift ;;

  fi

  dispatch_managed_execution "$harness" "$issue_id"
  exit $?
}

      *) shift ;;

  dispatch_managed_execution "$harness" "${issue_id:-none}"
  exit $?
}

# Seed the built-in tracker with N ready issues.
seed_tracker() {
  local root="$1" count="${2:-0}"
  mkdir -p "$root/work/.ddx"
  local i
  for ((i = 0; i < count; i++)); do
    printf '{"id":"hx-loop-%d","title":"loop issue %d","issue_type":"task","status":"open","priority":2,"labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:build"],"parent":"","spec-id":"","description":"","design":"","acceptance":"","dependencies":[],"owner":"","notes":"","execution-eligible":true,"superseded-by":"","replaces":"","created_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z"}\n' "$i" "$i"

}

# Run helix inside the workspace, with all env wired up.
run_helix() {
