  local cmd="${1:-help}"; shift || true
  (
    cd "$root/work"
    HOME="$root/home" \
    PATH="$root/bin:$PATH" \
    MOCK_STATE_ROOT="$root/state" \
    HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT="$repo_root/workflows" \
    HELIX_FORCE_EPHEMERAL=1 \
    HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT="codex" \
    HELIX_ALT_AGENT="none" \
    HELIX_DIRECT_AGENT=0 \
    DDX_BEAD_DIR="$root/work/.ddx" \
    DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK=1 \
    bash "$repo_root/scripts/helix" "$cmd" --quiet "$@"
  )
}

# Capture stdout+stderr separately for assertions.
run_helix_capture() {
  local root="$1"; shift
  local cmd="${1:-help}"; shift || true
  (
    cd "$root/work"
    HOME="$root/home" \
    PATH="$root/bin:$PATH" \
    MOCK_STATE_ROOT="$root/state" \
    HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT="$repo_root/workflows" \
    HELIX_FORCE_EPHEMERAL=1 \
    HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT="codex" \
    HELIX_ALT_AGENT="none" \
    HELIX_DIRECT_AGENT=0 \
    DDX_BEAD_DIR="$root/work/.ddx" \
    DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK=1 \
    bash "$repo_root/scripts/helix" "$cmd" --quiet "$@" 2>&1
  )
}

# ── Test runner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

run_test() {
  local name="$1"; shift
  local start_time
  start_time="$(date +%s.%N)"
  (( test_count++ ))
  printf '%s\n' "  [$test_count] $name"
  if "$@" 2>&1; then
    local elapsed
    elapsed="$(date +%s.%N)"
    printf '    ✓ passed (%.2fs)\n' "$(echo "$elapsed - $start_time" | bc)"
  else
    printf '    ✗ FAILED\n'
    exit 1
  fi
}

# =====================================================================
# FIXTURES
# =====================================================================

# ── (a) no_ready_work ───────────────────────────────────────────────

test_no_ready_work_exits_cleanly() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 0  # no ready work

  local stdout stderr rc
  # No issues → pick_issue returns empty → execute-loop returns {"processed":0}
  # HELIX should stop after one check-and-drain pass with 0 cycles.
  # Patch the mock so pick_issue also sees no issues.
  # (The workspace is already seeded with 0 issues.)

  stderr="$(run_helix_capture "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2>/dev/null)" || true

  # Should NOT crash.
  [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]] || true

  # No "implementation" dispatched.
  local ddx_calls
  ddx_calls="$(cat "$root/state/ddx-calls.log" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"

  # execute-loop should be called once (drain pass) but no dispatch.
  assert_contains "$ddx_calls" "execute-loop" \
    "execute-loop should be called even when no work is ready (drain attempt)"
  assert_not_contains "$ddx_calls" "dispatch" \
    "no_ready_work: execute-loop should return without dispatching"

  rm -rf "$root"
}

# ── (b) single success ──────────────────────────────────────────────

test_single_success_applies_post_cycle() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 1

  # Script execute-loop to return a single success.
  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "bead_id": "hx-loop-0",
      "status": "success",
      "duration_ms": 1500
    }
  ]
}
EOF

  run_helix "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align >/dev/null

  local ddx_calls
  ddx_calls="$(cat "$root/state/ddx-calls.log")"

  # execute-loop should be invoked.
  assert_contains "$ddx_calls" "execute-loop hx-loop-0" \
    "single success: execute-loop should be called with the bead"

  # Post-cycle: the bead should be closed.
  local bead_status
  bead_status="$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2>/dev/null \
    | ddx jq -r '.status')"
  assert_eq "closed" "$bead_status" \
    "single success: HELIX should close the bead post-cycle"

  rm -rf "$root"
}

# ── (c) mixed success/failure ───────────────────────────────────────

test_mixed_success_failure_per_bead_post_cycle() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 2

  # Script execute-loop to return mixed results.
  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 2,
  "results": [
    {
      "bead_id": "hx-loop-0",
      "status": "success",
      "duration_ms": 1200
    },
    {
      "bead_id": "hx-loop-1",
      "status": "failure",
      "duration_ms": 3000,
      "error": "implementation returned non-zero"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

  run_helix "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align >/dev/null

  local ddx_calls
  ddx_calls="$(cat "$root/state/ddx-calls.log")"

  assert_contains "$ddx_calls" "execute-loop" \
    "mixed: execute-loop should be called"

  # hx-loop-0 should be closed; hx-loop-1 should NOT be closed.
  local s0 s1
  s0="$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')"
  s1="$(ddx bead show hx-loop-1 --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')"
  assert_eq "closed" "$s0" "mixed: successful bead should be closed"
  assert_eq "open"   "$s1" "mixed: failed bead should NOT be auto-closed by HELIX"

  rm -rf "$root"
}

# ── (d) retry-after cooldown ─────────────────────────────────────────

test_retry_after_cooldown_does_not_unclaim() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 1

  # Script execute-loop to return a retry-after result.
  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "bead_id": "hx-loop-0",
      "status": "failure",
      "duration_ms": 800,
      "retry_after": "2029-12-31T23:59:59Z",
      "reason": "execution timed out"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

  run_helix "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align >/dev/null

  local ddx_calls
  ddx_calls="$(cat "$root/state/ddx-calls.log")"
  assert_contains "$ddx_calls" "execute-loop hx-loop-0" \
    "retry-after: execute-loop should be called"

  # The bead should NOT be auto-closed (cooldown is DDx's job).
  local bead_status
  bead_status="$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')"
  assert_eq "open" "$bead_status" \
    "retry-after: HELIX should not auto-close a cooldown bead"

  # The bead should remain claimed (HELIX should not unclaim it either).
  local claimed_pid
  claimed_pid="$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r '."claimed-pid" // empty')"
  #claimed_pid may be empty if unclaimed; the key assertion is it should NOT
  #be forcibly unclaimed by HELIX (safe_unclaim guards closed beads only).
  #Since execute-loop owns the retry_after state, HELIX should leave it alone.
  assert_not_contains "$ddx_calls" "unclaim" \
    "retry-after: HELIX should not explicitly unclaim a cooldown bead"

  rm -rf "$root"
}

# ── (e) preserve_ref ────────────────────────────────────────────────

test_preserve_ref_surfaces_without_land() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 1

  # Script execute-loop to return a preserve_ref result.
  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "bead_id": "hx-loop-0",
      "status": "success",
      "preserve_ref": "feat/keep-this-work",
      "duration_ms": 900
    }
  ]
}
EOF

  local stderr
  stderr="$(run_helix_capture "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2>/dev/null)"

  # The preserve_ref should be surfaced in the output.
  assert_contains "$stderr" "preserve_ref" \
    "preserve_ref: HELIX should surface the preserve_ref from execute-loop"
  assert_contains "$stderr" "feat/keep-this-work" \
    "preserve_ref: HELIX should surface the preserve_ref value"

  # The bead should still be closed (preserve_ref is informational).
  local bead_status
  bead_status="$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')"
  assert_eq "closed" "$bead_status" \
    "preserve_ref: bead should be closed (preserve_ref does not block land)"

  rm -rf "$root"
}

# ── (f) tolerant parsing ─────────────────────────────────────────────

test_tolerant_parsing_unknown_fields_ignored() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 1

  # Script execute-loop to return results with completely unknown fields
  # at both top-level and per-result level.  HELIX must not error.
  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 1,
  "totally_unknown_top_field": "should be ignored",
  "another_nonsense": 99999,
  "results": [
    {
      "bead_id": "hx-loop-0",
      "status": "success",
      "duration_ms": 500,
      "zzz_custom_agent_field": "hello",
      "ddx_internal_trace_id": "abc123",
      "nested_junk": {"foo": "bar", "baz": [1,2,3]}
    }
  ]
}
EOF

  local stderr rc=0
  stderr="$(run_helix_capture "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2>/dev/null)" || rc=$?

  # Should exit cleanly even with unknown fields.
  [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]] || {
    # If it fails, stderr should NOT be a JSON parse error.
    if [[ "$stderr" == *"JSON"* || "$stderr" == *"parse"* || "$stderr" == *"unexpected"* ]]; then
      printf 'Unexpected parse error from tolerant fixture:\n%s\n' "$stderr" >&2
      fail "tolerant parsing: unknown fields caused a parse error"
    fi
  }

  # Execute-loop should still have been invoked.
  local ddx_calls
  ddx_calls="$(cat "$root/state/ddx-calls.log")"
  assert_contains "$ddx_calls" "execute-loop" \
    "tolerant parsing: execute-loop should be called"

  rm -rf "$root"
}

# ── (g) strict required fields ──────────────────────────────────────

test_missing_bead_id_causes_structured_error() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 1

  # Script execute-loop to return a result missing bead_id.
  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "status": "success",
      "duration_ms": 500
    }
  ]
}
EOF

  local stderr rc=0
  stderr="$(run_helix_capture "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2>/dev/null)" || rc=$?

  # Should fail (missing bead_id is a required field).
  [[ "$rc" -ne 0 ]] || {
    # If it accidentally passes, the behavior must be documented:
    # either it produces a structured error in output OR it closes nothing.
    # The contract requires a clear structured error, not silent pass.
    local bead_status
    bead_status="$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')"
    if [[ "$bead_status" == "closed" ]]; then
      fail "strict required fields: missing bead_id must cause an error, not silently close the bead"
    fi
    fail "strict required fields: missing bead_id must cause non-zero exit or structured error"
  }

  # Error output should reference the missing field or bead_id.
  if [[ "$stderr" != *"bead_id"* && "$stderr" != *"required"* && "$stderr" != *"missing"* ]]; then
    printf 'Expected error about missing bead_id; got:\n%s\n' "$stderr" >&2
    fail "strict required fields: error should mention the missing field (bead_id)"
  fi

  rm -rf "$root"
}

test_missing_status_causes_structured_error() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 1

  # Script execute-loop to return a result missing status.
  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "bead_id": "hx-loop-0",
      "duration_ms": 500
    }
  ]
}
EOF

  local stderr rc=0
  stderr="$(run_helix_capture "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align 2>/dev/null)" || rc=$?

  # Should fail (missing status is a required field).
  [[ "$rc" -ne 0 ]] || {
    local bead_status
    bead_status="$(ddx bead show hx-loop-0 --json 2>/dev/null | ddx jq -r '.status')"
    if [[ "$bead_status" == "closed" ]]; then
      fail "strict required fields: missing status must cause an error, not silently close the bead"
    fi
    fail "strict required fields: missing status must cause non-zero exit or structured error"
  }

  # Error output should reference the missing field or status.
  if [[ "$stderr" != *"status"* && "$stderr" != *"required"* && "$stderr" != *"missing"* ]]; then
    printf 'Expected error about missing status; got:\n%s\n' "$stderr" >&2
    fail "strict required fields: error should mention the missing field (status)"
  fi

  rm -rf "$root"
}

# =====================================================================
# CONTRACT MATRIX — code-link resolution verification
# =====================================================================

# CONTRACT-001-audit.md rows with resolution='code-link:<path>':
# Each row's linked behavior is asserted below.  New-DDx-bead rows are
# skipped-pending.  HELIX-backport rows have in-harness fakes.
#
# Skipped-pending (new-DDx-bead resolutions, not yet shipped):
#   • ddx-3e7f8213  — gate machinery (evaluateRequiredGates) not yet wired
#   • ddx-91bc770a  — dirty-tree checkpointing not yet automatic
#
# HELIX-backport fakes (in-harness):
#   • Post-cycle close: covered by fixtures (b), (c)
#   • Retry suppression: covered by fixture (d)
#   • preserve_ref surfacing: covered by fixture (e)

test_contract_execute_loop_claims_execution_ready() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  seed_tracker "$root" 2

  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 1,
  "results": [
    {"bead_id": "hx-loop-0", "status": "success", "duration_ms": 100}
  ]
}
EOF

  run_helix "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align >/dev/null

  local ddx_calls
  ddx_calls="$(cat "$root/state/ddx-calls.log")"
  assert_contains "$ddx_calls" "execute-loop hx-loop-0" \
    "code-link execute-behaviour: execute-loop must be called for execution-ready beads"
  rm -rf "$root"
}

test_contract_execute_loop_filters_blocked() {
  local root
  root="$(make_workspace)"
  # One open but dep-blocked bead; one ready bead.
  mkdir -p "$root/work/.ddx"
  cat >"$root/work/.ddx/beads.jsonl" <<'EOF'
{"id":"hx-blocked","title":"blocked","issue_type":"task","status":"open","priority":2,"labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:build"],"parent":"","spec-id":"","description":"","design":"","acceptance":"","dependencies":["hx-nonexistent"],"owner":"","notes":"","execution-eligible":false,"superseded-by":"","replaces":"","created_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z"}
{"id":"hx-loop-0","title":"ready","issue_type":"task","status":"open","priority":2,"labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:build"],"parent":"","spec-id":"","description":"","design":"","acceptance":"","dependencies":[],"owner":"","notes":"","execution-eligible":true,"superseded-by":"","replaces":"","created_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z"}
EOF

  cat >"$root/state/execute-loop-response.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "processed": 1,
  "results": [
    {"bead_id": "hx-loop-0", "status": "success", "duration_ms": 100}
  ]
}
EOF

  run_helix "$root" run --no-auto-review --no-auto-align >/dev/null

  local ddx_calls
  ddx_calls="$(cat "$root/state/ddx-calls.log")"
  # Blocked bead should NOT be executed.
  assert_not_contains "$ddx_calls" "hx-blocked" \
    "contract: blocked beads should not appear in execute-loop calls"
  assert_contains "$ddx_calls" "hx-loop-0" \
    "contract: execution-eligible beads should be passed to execute-loop"
  rm -rf "$root"
}

# =====================================================================
# MAIN
# =====================================================================

main() {
  printf '\n=== helix-loop-integration.sh ===\n'
  printf 'Contract: execute-loop --json response shapes\n\n'

  local failed=0

  run_test "no_ready_work: exits cleanly, no cycle counted" \
    test_no_ready_work_exits_cleanly || failed=1

  run_test "single_success: HELIX applies post-cycle to the bead" \
    test_single_success_applies_post_cycle || failed=1

  run_test "mixed: per-bead post-cycle on success and failure" \
    test_mixed_success_failure_per_bead_post_cycle || failed=1

  run_test "retry_after: HELIX does not unclaim or blame cooldown bead" \
    test_retry_after_cooldown_does_not_unclaim || failed=1

  run_test "preserve_ref: HELIX surfaces it without attempting land" \
    test_preserve_ref_surfaces_without_land || failed=1

  run_test "tolerant_parsing: unknown fields ignored without error" \
    test_tolerant_parsing_unknown_fields_ignored || failed=1

  run_test "strict_missing_bead_id: causes structured error" \
    test_missing_bead_id_causes_structured_error || failed=1

  run_test "strict_missing_status: causes structured error" \
    test_missing_status_causes_structured_error || failed=1

  printf '\n--- Contract matrix: code-link resolution verification ---\n'

  run_test "contract: execute-loop called for execution-ready beads" \
    test_contract_execute_loop_claims_execution_ready || failed=1

  run_test "contract: execute-loop filters dep-blocked beads" \
    test_contract_execute_loop_filters_blocked || failed=1

  # Skipped-pending (new-DDx-bead resolutions not yet shipped):
  if [[ "${REPORT_SKIPPED:-0}" == "1" ]]; then
    printf '\n--- Skipped-pending (new-DDx-bead) ---\n'
    printf '  SKIP ddx-3e7f8213: gate machinery not yet wired (evaluateRequiredGates)\n'
    printf '  SKIP ddx-91bc770a: dirty-tree checkpointing not yet automatic\n'
  fi

  printf '\n'
  if (( failed )); then
    printf 'FAILED (%d test(s))\n' "$failed"
    exit 1
  else
    printf 'All tests passed (%d total)\n' "$test_count"
    exit 0
  fi
}

make_workspace() {
  cd "$repo_root" 2>/dev/null || true
  local root
  root="$(mktemp -d)"
  mkdir -p "$root/work"
  make_mock_bin "$root"
  (
    cd "$root/work"
    git init -q
  )
  echo "$root"
}

# Seed the built-in tracker with N ready issues.
seed_tracker() {
  local root="$1" count="${2:-0}"
  mkdir -p "$root/work/.ddx"
  local i
  for ((i = 0; i < count; i++)); do
    printf '{"id":"hx-loop-%d","title":"loop issue %d","issue_type":"task","status":"open","priority":2,"labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:build"],"parent":"","spec-id":"","description":"","design":"","acceptance":"","dependencies":[],"owner":"","notes":"","execution-eligible":true,"superseded-by":"","replaces":"","created_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z"}\n' "$i" "$i"
  done > "$root/work/.ddx/beads.jsonl"
}

# Run helix inside the workspace, with all env wired up.
run_helix() {
  local root="$1"; shift
  local cmd="${1:-help}"; shift || true
  (
    cd "$root/work"
    HOME="$root/home" \
    PATH="$root/bin:$PATH" \

    HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT="$repo_root/workflows" \

    HELIX_ALT_AGENT="none" \
    HELIX_DIRECT_AGENT=0 \
    DDX_BEAD_DIR="$root/work/.ddx" \
    DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK=1 \
    bash "$repo_root/scripts/helix" "$cmd" --quiet "$@"
  )
}

# Seed the built-in tracker with N ready issues.
seed_tracker() {
  local root="$1" count="${2:-0}"
  mkdir -p "$root/work/.ddx"
  local i
  for ((i = 0; i < count; i++)); do
    printf '{"id":"hx-loop-%d","title":"loop issue %d","issue_type":"task","status":"open","priority":2,"labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:build"],"parent":"","spec-id":"","description":"","design":"","acceptance":"","dependencies":[],"owner":"","notes":"","execution-eligible":true,"superseded-by":"","replaces":"","created_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z"}\n' "$i" "$i"
  done > "$root/work/.ddx/beads.jsonl"
}

# Seed the built-in tracker with N ready issues.
seed_tracker() {
  local root="$1" count="${2:-0}"
  mkdir -p "$root/work/.ddx"
  local i
  for ((i = 0; i < count; i++)); do
    printf '{"id":"hx-loop-%d","title":"loop issue %d","issue_type":"task","status":"open","priority":2,"labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:build"],"parent":"","spec-id":"","description":"","design":"","acceptance":"","dependencies":[],"owner":"","notes":"","execution-eligible":true,"superseded-by":"","replaces":"","created_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2099-01-01T00:00:00Z"}\n' "$i" "$i"
  done > "$root/work/.ddx/beads.jsonl"
}

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:73
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 783ef571 adds new runtime logic in bin/helix to derive HELIX_ROOT from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or the wrapper path, but the deterministic verification surface still does not execute that wrapper. tests/validate-skills.sh only checks that bin/helix exists and is executable, and tests/test-install.sh exercises the copied HOME/.local/bin/helix launcher with HELIX_ROOT injected manually instead of the plugin wrapper path. That leaves the new delegation logic effectively code-inspection-only: a regression in bin/helix path resolution can slip through the documented plugin packaging lane while FEAT-004 and the implementation plan both require a bin/helix delegation test.
Suggested fix: Add a checked-in deterministic test that runs bin/helix through the plugin-style path, proves it sets HELIX_ROOT from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or wrapper resolution, and wire that check into the standard plugin packaging validation lane.","id":"helix-0d48272d","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:cli","area:testing"],"owner":"erik","parent":"helix-13cfe23f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"tests/validate-skills.sh","status":"closed","title":"correctness: add deterministic coverage for bin/helix delegation","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:41:13.812209756Z"}

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/meta.yml:57 and workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/template.md:62
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 839e82e removes `runbook` from `monitoring-setup.dependencies.requires`, but the artifact contract still assumes the runbook exists before this creation step. `monitoring-setup` remains `creation_order: 2` while `runbook` is `creation_order: 3`, yet the template still requires a `Runbook Links` section and the validation check still asks the document to reference the runbook. That leaves the deploy workflow internally inconsistent: the scheduler can now generate `monitoring-setup` before `runbook`, but the generated document is still asked to link to a later artifact.
Suggested fix: Make the metadata and template agree on whether `runbook` is an input. Either restore a backward-compatible prerequisite by reordering the artifacts, or keep the current ordering and relax the monitoring contract so incident response can be completed without pre-existing runbook links.","id":"helix-15eff7dc","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:artifacts","area:workflow"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/meta.yml","status":"closed","title":"integration: align monitoring-setup runbook contract with deploy ordering","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:22:34.084194356Z"}

40:{"acceptance":"HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance; contract template support exists in the workflow bundle; guidance covers non-HTTP protocol/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas; ADR/SD/TD/contract boundaries are unambiguous enough that a telemetry export contract can be placed without artifact-taxonomy confusion","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:58:27.330158522Z","description":"<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/phases/02-design/README.md — HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance</governing>
</context-digest>

HELIX design guidance already treats contracts as a first-class artifact category (`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`), and the design gate expects contract docs to exist, but the workflow bundle does not currently ship a dedicated contract artifact template/prompt/meta alongside solution-design, technical-design, ADR, spike, and PoC templates. At the same time, live HELIX repos are using both `API-*` and `CONTRACT-*` identifiers for documents in the contracts directory. This leaves contract-shaped specs underspecified exactly when we need them for reusable protocol/interface definitions such as OpenTelemetry telemetry capture standards, DDx/HELIX boundary contracts, execution-document conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other cross-project schemas that should be shareable without dragging along feature-level or solution-level baggage.

Request: add an explicit first-class contract artifact type to HELIX design-phase tooling and documentation. The artifact must be suitable for normative external interface specifications, not just HTTP APIs. It should support protocol/data/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas, cross-project ownership boundaries, authoring conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other reusable contracts. The artifact definition should make clear that ADRs carry the decision/rationale (WHY), solution designs carry the feature-level approach (HOW for a feature), technical designs carry one bounded slice, and contracts carry the authoritative field-by-field/schema-level/interface-level specification that another team could implement against directly.

Requirements from this discussion:
1. Define clear selection rules for when to use ADR vs Solution Design vs Technical Design vs Contract.
2. Ship a contract template/prompt/meta in the HELIX workflow bundle.
3. Clarify identifier and naming guidance: whether `API-*` remains a subtype, whether `CONTRACT-*` is the canonical prefix, or whether both are supported with explicit scope boundaries.
4. Make the contract artifact suitable for non-HTTP surfaces, including telemetry/export contracts.
5. Require contracts to carry exact keys/fields, types, units, enums, precedence rules, formulas, error semantics, examples, and validation checklists where relevant.
6. Ensure the design gate/enforcer language matches the chosen artifact naming and template support.
7. Preserve the ability for contracts to be shared across projects without inheriting unrelated feature or solution baggage.
8. Document how contracts should be referenced from ADRs and solution designs so the normative schema lives in one place rather than being duplicated across artifacts.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"tier=cheap harness= model= probe=no viable provider

Scope (narrow, by design): compare ONLY four fields per cycle — executed bead ids, terminal statuses, retry metadata (retry_after, preserve_ref), and operator-facing summary lines. Do NOT diff internal state, timings, or log ordering.

One live lane: on a clean working tree with a small real tracker subset (5-10 open beads), run both paths against the same frozen .ddx/beads.jsonl snapshot; assert same four-field outcome. Live lane runs once per CI on main; fixtures run on every PR.

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.implementation-plan — This build plan defines the current HELIX execution landscape: which features are implemented, which have remaining work, what the governing artifacts are, and what ordering constraints keep implementation aligned with the authority stack.</governing>
</context-digest>

The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

data.text: I’m making the decomposition as a tracker-only operation: one small child bead with the same labels/spec-id and a deterministic JSON-lines verification command. After that I’ll write the required no_changes rationale in the execution bundle and stop.

input.command:
/bin/zsh -lc 'description=$(cat <<'"'EOF'
Parent helix-495f0d72 was too large for direct execution because the prompt embedded the full existing asciinema cast. This child is the execution-ready slice.

Task: refresh the checked-in helix-concerns asciinema recording so the public demo shows the fresh-eyes review drift finding bead with a scope-appropriate area label. The expected finding is for tests/drift.test.ts importing vitest instead of bun:test, so area:api is the appropriate label from the demo concerns file.

In-scope files:
- docs/demos/helix-concerns/recordings/helix-concerns-20260406-180259.cast
- docs/demos/helix-concerns/agent-dictionary/*.json only if needed to regenerate the cast consistently
- docs/demos/helix-concerns/fixtures/** only if needed to keep replay deterministic

Out-of-scope:
- Other demo recordings
- Website layout or content outside generated references to this recording
- HELIX/DDx runtime behavior changes

Preserve asciinema v2 JSON-lines format. Do not hand-edit unrelated transcript content.
EOF
)
acceptance="'$(cat <<'"'EOF'
"'`python3 - <<'"'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('docs/demos/helix-concerns/recordings/helix-concerns-20260406-180259.cast')
lines = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines()
assert lines, 'cast is empty'
for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
    json.loads(line)
outputs = []
for line in lines[1:]:
    event = json.loads(line)
    if len(event) >= 3 and event[1] == 'o':
        outputs.append(event[2])
text = ''.join(outputs)
assert 'drift: tests/drift.test.ts imports vitest instead of bun:test' in text
assert 'review-finding' in text
assert 'area:api' in text
PY"'` passes, proving the checked-in cast is valid JSON lines and displays the drift finding with a scope-appropriate area:* label.
EOF
)
ddx bead create "Refresh helix-concerns cast with area-labeled drift finding" '"\\
  --type task \\
  --priority 1 \\
  --parent helix-495f0d72 \\
  --labels helix,phase:build,area:demo,area:docs,area:site \\
  --set spec-id=docs/demos/helix-concerns/recordings/helix-concerns-20260406-180259.cast \\
  --description \""'$description" '"\\
  --acceptance \""'$acceptance"'

  "description": "\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision.
